message:
fix(vdbe): guard MakeRecord precomputed-header fast path against column-count mismatch

The OP_MakeRecord hot path trusts P4::PrecomputedHeader metadata to drive
serialization without re-inspecting the actual register range. If the
precomputed header's column_count() diverges from the opcode's p2 field
(the authoritative source-of-truth for register span), the fast path would
serialize the wrong number of columns — either truncating data or reading
past the intended register window.

This commit adds a guard: `header.column_count() == n_cols`. When the
invariant holds, the existing zero-copy precomputed-header path runs as
before. When it doesn't (e.g. after a schema change that invalidated cached
program metadata), execution falls through to the generic register-walking
path that derives serial types dynamically.

Also hoists the `n_cols` computation (from op.p2) above the match so it's
available to both the precomputed and generic branches without duplication.

Test: test_make_record_precomputed_header_falls_back_on_column_count_mismatch
emits a 3-column precomputed header but sets p2=2, verifying the engine
produces a correct 2-column record via the fallback path.

Benchmark update: make_record bench now uses VdbeEngine::new_with_execution_cx
to match the current engine constructor signature (requires Cx and PageSize).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
